+    // "to" may no longer exist if it was backed up. Use symlink metadata here so

content.text:
Implemented the `mv` fix in `src/uu/mv/src/mv.rs`.

Summary:
- The bug was caused by using `Path::exists()` / `Path::is_dir()`, which follow symlinks.
- When moving a symlink-to-directory over another symlink-to-directory with `-T`, uutils treated the destination as the target directory itself and rejected it as “Directory not empty”.
- Updated the directory replacement check to use `symlink_metadata()`, so it only treats the path as a directory when the path itself is a directory, not when it is a symlink pointing to one.
- This matches GNU `mv` behavior: the destination symlink is replaced, while the pointed-to directory remains intact.

Validation performed:
- Added a temporary regression test reproducing the failure.
- Confirmed it failed before the fix.
- Applied the fix.
- Confirmed the regression test passed.
- Removed the temporary test change as requested.
- Ran a manual validation scenario with directory symlinks and confirmed success.

Remaining working tree changes:
- `src/uu/mv/src/mv.rs` modified with the fix.
- There is an existing untracked `reports/` directory that I did not touch.

Implemented the `mv` fix in `src/uu/mv/src/mv.rs`.

Summary:
- The bug was caused by using `Path::exists()` / `Path::is_dir()`, which follow symlinks.
- When moving a symlink-to-directory over another symlink-to-directory with `-T`, uutils treated the destination as the target directory itself and rejected it as “Directory not empty”.
- Updated the directory replacement check to use `symlink_metadata()`, so it only treats the path as a directory when the path itself is a directory, not when it is a symlink pointing to one.
- This matches GNU `mv` behavior: the destination symlink is replaced, while the pointed-to directory remains intact.

Validation performed:

- Confirmed it failed before the fix.
